title: Ensure Workspaces images are not older than 90 days.

input_row.title: Ensure Workspaces images are not older than 90 days.
input_row.description: WorkSpaces images should not have a creation time stamp over 90 days.
input_row.rationale: WorkSpaces images require Operating system patches to be applied and updated and by confirming the creation date is not over 90 days old can help ensure that updates are being applied.
input_row.audit: Perform the following to determine the age of WorkSpaces images. From the Console: 1. Login to the WorkSpaces dashboard at https://console.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/ 2. In the left pane click Images. 3. Review the Created date and confirm that all images are newer than 90 days. If any images are older than 90 days refer to the remediation procedure below.
input_row.remediation: To create a custom image From the Console: Note - If you are still connected to the WorkSpace, disconnect. 1. Log in to the WorkSpaces console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/workspaces/ 2. In the left pane, choose WorkSpaces. 3. Select the WorkSpace and choose Actions, Create Image. - A message displays, prompting you to restart your WorkSpace before continuing. Restarting your WorkSpace updates your Amazon WorkSpaces software to the latest version. Once you have restarted your WorkSpace, repeat Step 4 of this procedure. 5. Click Next. 6. Enter an image name and a description. 7. Click Create Image. While the image is being created, the status of the WorkSpace is Suspended and the WorkSpace is unavailable. In the left pane, click Images. The image is complete when the status of the WorkSpace changes to Available.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_workspaces_image_creation_date_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the boto3 SDK to interact with the AWS WorkSpaces API.
- Specifically, the `describe_images()` method will be used to retrieve a list of WorkSpaces images and their creation dates.
  
- Example API Call:
  ```python
  import boto3

  # Create a WorkSpaces client
  client = boto3.client('workspaces')

  # Retrieve all images
  response = client.describe_images()

  # Example of checking creation dates
  for image in response['Images']:
      creation_date = image['Created']
      # Perform comparison logic to check if age is over 90 days
  ```

- AWS CLI Command:
  ```bash
  aws workspaces describe-images
  ```

- The resources to enumerate will include WorkSpaces images in the specified account and region.
- The configuration property to validate is the `Created` date of each image.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: The `Created` date of every WorkSpaces image is within the last 90 days.
  - Fail: Any WorkSpaces image has a `Created` date older than 90 days. 

This automated approach ensures efficient and consistent compliance checking of the WorkSpaces images against the defined CIS benchmark control.
